articles.title: China’s Shenzhou-21 Crew Returns to Earth
articles.summary: China’s three-man Shenzhou-21 (SZ-21) crew returned to Earth today after 210 days aboard the Tiangong space station. They landed not in the SZ-21 spacecraft, however, but SZ-22. SZ-22 was launched […]

articles.title: Space Force awards SpaceX $4.16 billion to build satellite network for airborne target tracking
articles.summary:
The low-orbit satellite constellation is intended to track aircraft, cruise missiles and other airborne threats

articles.title: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
articles.summary: Engineers at NASA’s Goddard Space Flight Center in Greenbelt, Maryland, have completed their final inspection of a key element for the agency’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope: the primary mirror. This 7.9-foot (2.4-meter) mirror will collect and focus light from cosmic objects near and far, helping Roman capture stunning panoramas of space. “The Roman engineering […]

articles.title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
articles.summary: This NASA Hubble Space Telescope image released on May 27, 2026, features the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, roughly 12,000 light-years in diameter and some 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. The galaxy’s low surface brightness makes it appear as a faint, starry swarm behind brighter foreground stars that are easily recognized by their diffraction spikes. […]

articles.title: This Month at ESA: May 2026
articles.summary: What did space deliver for Europe this month? From asteroid flybys to Mars landing tests, new discoveries from Webb and Hubble, and ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot capturing shooting stars from orbit, here’s your monthly roundup from the European Space Agency.

articles.summary:

articles.title: Bellatrix and TelePIX plan 2028 air-breathing VLEO imaging demonstration
articles.summary:
South Korean optical payload developer TelePIX and Indian propulsion specialist Bellatrix Aerospace have teamed up on a very low Earth orbit geospatial demonstration mission slated for 2028.

articles.summary: "I hope that it makes it far enough away from the pad that it does not cause pad damage."
